Added damma (ُ) at the end to show this word is in the nominative case

steps.m: a group of people who stand together (community/nation)
steps.n: The root meaning of standing firmly is transformed into a noun for people who stand together as one community
The kasra at the end shows this is a possessed noun - these are my people

steps.m: O my people! (calling out to them)
steps.n: Adding يٰ at the start shows we are calling out to these people. This word appears when Aaron is speaking to his people about the golden calf they made.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Adding ما changes the meaning from just emphasis to restriction (only). This word introduces what Harun previously said to his people.

steps.m: to test or try someone's faith through hardship

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it
Added تُم at the end to show this happened to multiple people being spoken to
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning by/with
Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show what/who is being referred to. Here it refers back to being tested (فُتِنتُم) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after ه which is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning
This is a basic emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: and indeed (connects to فُتِنتُم - you were tested)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before. This connects to how they were tested with the calf.

steps.m: to care for, raise, nurture, develop

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete lordship/mastery
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of إِنَّ in the ayah
Added كُمُ at the end to show this is your (plural) Lord

steps.m: The Most Merciful, full of mercy
steps.n: The ان ending makes it mean 'full of mercy'
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject or after 'the'
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific. The ر is doubled to show emphasis since it comes after ال. This is one of Allah's names meaning 'The Most Merciful'.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern
Added اِتَّ pattern to show deliberate, intensive following
Changed from 'he followed' to 'you follow!' by changing the vowels to make it a command to one person
Added و to make it plural command

steps.m: all of you follow me! (plural command)
steps.n: Added نِ to show 'me' as object

steps.m: so all of you follow me! (plural command)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to mean 'so/then' and ى at end for emphasis
Connected the root letters with vowels to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he obeyed' to 'all of you obey!' by adding -ū ending and changing middle vowel to ī
Added أَ at start to make it a command form

steps.m: and obey! (all of you) (command related to أَمْرِى - my command)
steps.n: Added 'wa' (and) at the start to connect it to what came before, showing this command follows another command
Changed from basic root meaning 'to command' into the noun 'a command' by adding marks (fatha and sukun)
Added kasra at end to show this word is possessed by something
Added ى at the end to show this belongs to me
Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action. This word is connected to لَن نَّبْرَحَ (we will not cease) which comes after it.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it impossible in the future. Here it negates the verb يَرْجِعَ (return), showing the people are saying Musa will not return.

steps.m: to leave or move away from a place

steps.n: Added vowels to make it into a past tense verb meaning 'he left'
Changed the first vowel to sukun to prepare for present tense form
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This verb is negated by لَن before it in the ayah, making it mean 'we will not leave'

steps.m: we will leave/move away (emphatically negated by لَن)
steps.n: The ن gets doubled (shadda) after combining with the previous letter ل in لَن
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or against something else. In this ayah, it connects to the next word to show what they are devoted to.
Added هِ at the end to mean 'him', with ي as a connecting letter. This shows who they are staying devoted to in the ayah.

steps.m: to stay, remain, or devote oneself to something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Changed the verb into a participle showing who does the action
Makes the word plural - means more than one person who stays devoted
Adds the accusative case ending
This is a preposition that shows a limit or endpoint in time or space. The shadda (ّ) on ت makes the sound stronger to emphasize reaching that endpoint.
The ىٰ (alif maqsura) at the end shows this word connects to what comes after it. Here it connects to يَرْجِعَ meaning 'until he returns'.
Changes from basic root to present tense 'he' form by adding يَ at start
Adding fatha at end makes it 'that he' instead of just 'he'
This is a preposition that shows movement or direction towards something

steps.m: to us (referring to the people speaking to Moses)
steps.n: Adding نَا shows this is happening to 'us'. In this ayah, 'us' refers to the people speaking to Moses, saying they won't leave until he returns to them.
This is the base form of the name Moses, borrowed from Egyptian/Hebrew where it meant 'drawn from water', referring to how Pharaoh's daughter found him
Added the vowel marks to show how to pronounce the name correctly
Added the alif maqsurah (ى) with an overextension mark (ٰ) at the end, which is how this name is typically written in the Quran
Added vowel marks to make the basic past tense form. This word is directed at Harun, as Musa is speaking to him.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs with و in the middle
This is a proper name that doesn't come from an Arabic root
Adding يٰ at the beginning shows someone is calling out to Aaron

steps.m: O Aaron! (being called, as the subject of مَا مَنَعَكَ)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows Aaron is the subject of the question 'What prevented you?' (مَا مَنَعَكَ)
This is a question word used by Moses to ask Aaron about what prevented him from acting. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'what prevented you' (مَا مَنَعَكَ)
Added fatha (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he prevented you (object of رَأَيْتَ - you saw)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show that 'you' is receiving the action of preventing. This word is connected to رَأَيْتَ (you saw) in the ayah.

steps.m: when (connecting to رَأَيْتَهُمْ - you saw them)
steps.n: This is a word that shows when something happened in the past. In this ayah, it connects what Harun did to when he saw them going astray.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb
Added تَ suffix to show 'you' did the action. The ى changes back to يْ before adding تَ.
Added هُمْ suffix meaning 'them' (masculine plural)
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he' form - like adding 'ed' in English
When two same letters come together at the end, they combine with a shadda
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
Starting with the first part أَنْ which shows something is about to happen
Added لَا which means 'not' to show negation
When أَنْ and لَا come together, the ن combines with ل to make a stronger ل sound (shown by ّ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ت at start to show 'you' are doing the action
Added a strong ت sound (ّ) and ِ to show doing the action with extra care and attention

steps.m: you follow me carefully (connected to أَلَّا meaning 'that not')
steps.n: Added نِ at the end to show the action is being done to 'me'. This word comes after أَلَّا which means 'that not', so together they mean 'that you not follow me'

steps.m: to disobey or rebel against authority

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows from something before it

steps.m: did you then disobey? (questioning أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question. This is questioning why the person didn't follow (أَلَّا تَتَّبِعَنِ)
Changed from basic root letters to a noun by adding the vowels 'a' and 'sukoon', making it mean 'a command'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is affected by a preposition
Adds possessive pronoun ى (my) to create the meaning 'my command'
Added fatha marks to show this happened in the past and was done by 'he'
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ because of weak letter و changing to ا

steps.m: to build or establish family relationships

steps.n: Root meaning 'build/establish' became the common noun for 'son' - like building a family
Added يا (yaa) at beginning to call out to someone - like saying 'Hey son!' or 'O son!'
Added the word for mother (umm) to make it more specific - like saying 'O son of (my) mother!'
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb تَأْخُذْ (take/grab) into a negative command meaning 'do not take/grab'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to make it present tense
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. This word is part of Moses speaking to Aaron, telling him not to grab his beard.
Added vowels and ة to change from the root meaning 'facial hair' to mean 'a specific beard'
Added بِ to show this is being grabbed/held. This word is being acted on by the verb تَأْخُذْ (take/grab).
Added ى to show this is my beard. The word is still being acted on by the verb تَأْخُذْ (take/grab).
This is a negative particle that makes something negative, like adding 'not' or 'don't' in English
Added و at the beginning to connect this negative command with what came before. This word connects to the verb تَأْخُذْ (take), making it 'and don't take'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is being done with/by something. This word connects to لَا تَأْخُذْ (do not take/grab) in this ayah.
Added kasra at the end to show this head belongs to someone
Added ى at the end to show this is 'my' head. In this ayah, Aaron is telling Moses not to grab his (Aaron's) head.
This is the basic particle that adds emphasis to a statement, like adding 'indeed' or 'verily' in English

steps.m: indeed I (connected to خَشِيتُ - I feared)
steps.n: Added ي at the end to show this is about 'me/I'. The word connects to خَشِيتُ (I feared) to say 'Indeed, I feared...'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is about feeling afraid in the past

steps.m: I became afraid (of أَن تَقُولَ - that you would say)
steps.n: Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did this action. This word connects to أَن تَقُولَ (that you would say) which explains what the speaker was afraid of.
This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what was feared - it connects 'I feared' (خَشِيتُ) with what follows. Like saying 'I feared THAT you would say...'
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he said' in the past tense
The past tense 'said' changes to present tense 'saying' by changing the vowels. The middle letter ا goes back to its original و.
Added تَ at the beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this is after 'that' or similar words
The shadda (ّ) on the middle letter makes the splitting action stronger and more forceful
Adding ت at the end changes 'he' to 'you'. In this ayah, Musa is speaking to his brother Harun about dividing the Children of Israel.
Changes from verb 'to separate' into noun meaning 'between'

steps.m: between (acted on by فَرَّقْتَ - you divided)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb فَرَّقْتَ (you divided)

steps.m: to build or establish a foundation

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to create basic past tense verb form
Added the elongated ى and ٓ to show proper pronunciation at the end of the word
This is the first part of the compound name, coming from ancient Hebrew meaning 'servant' or 'wrestler'
The alif is written with a special mark (alif maqsurah with madd) to show it's a long vowel
Added the second part 'il' meaning 'God' in ancient Hebrew, making the complete name mean 'servant/wrestler of God'
This is a negative particle that makes actions in the past not happen. It's like adding 'did not' in English.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement, like adding 'and' in English
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ت at start to change from 'he' to 'you'
Added sukun at end to make it jussive mood
Changed from the root meaning 'to say' into the noun 'saying/speech' by adding vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon)
Added ى at the end to show it belongs to 'me'. This is what wasn't followed in the sentence.

steps.m: he said (followed by his actual words)
steps.n: Added fathas to make it past tense. This word is followed by what he actually said.
The و (weak letter) changes to ا, which is very common in Arabic verbs
This is the question word 'what' used to ask about something. Here it's asking about the person's matter/situation.

steps.m: so what (asking about خَطْبُكَ - your matter)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this question follows from something that happened before. It's like saying 'So, what's your deal?' in English after seeing someone do something.
Changes the root letters for 'speaking' into a noun meaning 'important matter' by adding the vowels َ (fatha) and ْ (sukoon)
Adds ُ to show this word is doing something in the sentence

steps.m: your matter/affair (being asked about by مَا/what)
steps.n: Adds كَ meaning 'your' at the end. In this ayah, this word is being asked about by مَا (what), forming the question 'What is your matter?'

steps.m: to stay awake at night, to converse at night
one who stays up at night
steps.n: Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action
Added doubled yaa with shadda at the end to show this person belongs to or comes from a specific place (Samaria)
Added يٰ at beginning to show we're calling out to this person, and added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject being addressed
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he said'. This is the person who is doing the speaking after بَصُرْتُ (I saw).
The ُ (damma) on the middle letter shows this is about gaining or having a quality of clear perception

steps.m: I became perceptive/insightful (about مَا لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ - what they did not perceive)
steps.n: The تُ ending changes 'he' to 'I' in the past tense. In this ayah, the speaker is explaining how they gained insight into something others missed.
The preposition بِ shows how something is done or what is used

steps.m: with what (referring to what they did not see)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to show this refers to something that was not seen. In this ayah, it connects to يَبْصُرُوا۟ (they did not see)
A particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَبْصُرُوا۟) to show it did not happen in the past. Like saying 'did not see' instead of 'see'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: they see/perceive (in لَمْ يَبْصُرُوا۟ - they did not see)
steps.n: Adding plural masculine suffix وا to indicate third person plural subject
Basic preposition showing how something is done

steps.m: with him/it (referring to what he saw)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ to show who/what the preposition affects. Here it refers back to what the person saw that others didn't see.
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide to show the vowel is elongated slightly
Connected the root letters and added fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Changed تُ at the end shows 'I' did the action, and puts a sukun (ْ) on the last root letter to connect smoothly

steps.m: so I grasped (in response to بَصُرْتُ - I saw)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this action happened as a result of or response to seeing something, connecting it to the previous word بَصُرْتُ (I saw)
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'a single grasp' by adding vowels
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of grasping. This refers to the handful of dust from the messenger's footprint that was taken.
Added tanween (ً) to show this is the direct object of the verb فَقَبَضْتُ (so I grasped)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. It cannot be broken down into smaller parts.
The doubled م (shown by ّ) makes the word stronger and more emphatic. This emphasis shows up because the word comes after a word ending in a similar sound.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into a noun meaning 'trace'
Added kasra at the end to show it's being used after a preposition
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added ال to make it specific. The ل combines with ر to make a doubled ر (shown by ّ)
Added kasra at the end to show this messenger is being described or belongs to something

steps.m: to throw or cast away forcefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed subject to 'I' by adding تُ at the end. The ذ gets sukun (ْ) because it comes before تُ
Added هَا which means 'it' or 'her' when referring to something feminine. In this verse, it refers to the golden pieces/jewelry that were thrown.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this action happened as a result of or after something else. In this verse, it shows this throwing happened after taking something from the messenger's footprint.
Starting with the basic comparison particle
Adding the demonstrative element to form 'that'
Adding كَ to complete the compound meaning 'like that'

steps.m: and like that (connected to فَنَبَذْتُهَا - so I threw it)
steps.n: Adding وَ to connect this phrase to the previous action mentioned in the verse

steps.m: to make something seem attractive or appealing

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the meaning more intense
Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is feminine (referring to نَفْسِى - my soul/self)
Added kasra to the preposition ل to prepare it for connection with the pronoun
Added the pronoun ى (me) to show who the action is for. This word connects to سَوَّلَتْ (enticed/suggested), showing who was enticed.
Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun. This word is doing the action of سَوَّلَتْ (made fair-seeming) in this ayah.
The ى (ya) at the end means 'my', showing the self belongs to me. This word is doing the action in the sentence.
Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding short 'a' vowels (fathas)
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from 'he went' to 'go!' by adding اِ at start and putting a stop mark (sukun) at end - this makes it a command
Added فَ to show this is a result of what came before. This is Musa commanding Samiri after discovering his role in making the golden calf.
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: so indeed (connects to قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ - he said, 'Go!')
steps.n: The فَ shows this follows from what came before it (God telling him to go)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added كَ at the end to show this is directed to one person (you)
A basic preposition showing location or containment. Here it connects 'in' to 'the life' to show what you will have during your life.
This root represents the basic concept of living or being alive
Added vowels to connect the letters and changed the final ي to ا because two ي letters can't come together
Added و and ة to make it into the noun 'life'
Added ال to make it specific ('the life') and ِ to show it's connected to another word in the sentence

steps.m: that (introduces what will be said in تَقُولَ)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it introduces what will be said, connecting to the verb تَقُولَ (you say/will say) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters and added fathas to make the past tense form
Changed from past tense (qāla) to present tense (qūlu) by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'u'
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Changed ending vowel to fatha to show this is dependent on something else
A negative particle that makes something forbidden or impossible. Here it combines with the word مِسَاسَ (touching) to mean 'no touching!' as part of a punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the word into a verbal noun (masdar) that names the action itself. This appears after لا (no) to form the phrase 'no touching!'
Added the final vowel mark to complete the word's pronunciation
This is a basic emphasis particle that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'truly' in English
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this statement with what came before it
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows purpose or direction
Added the pronoun كَ to show who the preposition is directed to. This whole phrase is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily) in the ayah.

steps.m: to promise or set a time/place

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic noun
Added مَ at beginning and changed middle vowel to show where/when something happens

steps.m: an appointed time/place (object of لَكَ - for you)
steps.n: Added tanween fatHa to show this is the object being given or promised to someone
Combined two particles together: لَ (for emphasis) and أَنْ (that)

steps.m: never/will not (makes تُخْلَفَهُۥ negative in future)
steps.n: The أ is dropped and the meaning changes to show a strong negative in the future. This word connects to تُخْلَفَهُۥ (you will break it) to make it mean 'you will never break it'.
The ل gets a shadda to show emphasis, making the negative meaning stronger

steps.m: to break (a promise), to fail to fulfill

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make 'he broke/failed' in past tense - like adding -ed in English
Changed from active (he did it) to passive (it was done to it) by changing vowels
Added تُ at beginning to change from 'it was' to 'you will be' - like changing from past to future

steps.m: you will fail to fulfill it (object of لَّن)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show the object being broken/unfulfilled. This verb comes after لَّن which makes it negative future.

steps.m: to look at or observe something carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding 'u' at start and making it a command
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and', and changed command alif to connecting alif
This is a preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it shows Moses is being told to look towards his false god.
The alif at the end is written with a small alif (ى) topped with an alif mark (ٰ). This is just a writing variation and doesn't change the meaning.
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) above the alif to show that the sound is stretched slightly when reading. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to worship or be devoted to

steps.n: Changed the root letters to make a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped', like changing 'worship' to 'god'
Adding kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to something else
Changed the alif (ا) to a small alif (ٰ) above the letter, showing this means 'god of' something
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to you. This word follows إِلَىٰٓ (to) in the ayah.
Added ى to create the base demonstrative word
Added a double letter mark (shadda) to make the word stronger and show it connects to something after it

steps.m: who/which/that (referring to your god that you remained devoted to)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning to create the relative pronoun. In this ayah, it refers back to the false god mentioned in إِلٰهِكَ (your god).
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he remained'

steps.m: you remained continuously (addressing the one who worshipped the calf)
steps.n: Added تَ to show 'you' did this action in the past. In this ayah, Allah is addressing the person who had been worshipping the calf idol.
Adding ْ (sukun) on the middle letter helps show this happened in the past
Starting with the basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ before adding a pronoun. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him/on it (connected to إِلٰهِكَ - your god)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who or what is being referred to. Here it refers back to إِلٰهِكَ (your god) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb

steps.m: one who stays devoted (describing the subject of ظَلْتَ - you remained)
steps.n: Changed from a past tense verb (he stayed) to a word meaning 'one who stays' - like changing 'devoted' to 'devotee'. This describes how the person remained (ظَلْتَ).
The ًا ending shows this word describes how the action of ظَلْتَ (you remained) was done
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Added shadda to show intensity and changed to command form
Added نُ to show 'we' as the doers
Added لَ to emphasize that this will definitely happen

steps.m: surely we will definitely burn intensely
steps.n: Added نَّ for extra emphasis

steps.m: surely we will definitely burn it intensely
steps.n: Added هُۥ to show what is being burned and merged the لَ with shadda for emphasis

A connecting word that shows one action happens after another with a time gap between them. Here it connects 'we will burn it' with 'we will scatter it', showing these actions happen one after the other.

steps.m: to scatter/disperse something into tiny pieces

steps.n: Added ن at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action, and changed middle vowel to kasra for present tense
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and future certainty
Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis, showing absolute certainty

steps.m: we will most certainly scatter it
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show what is being scattered (referring to the false god/idol mentioned earlier)
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows that something exists or happens within 'life'.

steps.m: to go towards, head in a direction
sea, ocean (from the idea of where everything flows towards)
steps.n: Added shadda to show emphasis and doubled middle letter. The meaning shifted from direction to sea because seas are where waters flow towards.
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific sea
Added kasra because it comes after the word فِي (in)

steps.m: to scatter or blow away completely

steps.n: Changes verb into noun that describes the action

steps.m: a complete scattering (object of لَنَنسِفَنَّهُ - we will surely scatter it)
steps.n: Added tanwin alif to show this describes how thoroughly the action will be done. This emphasizes that the scattering will be complete and total.
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it in the sentence.
Adding ما changes the meaning from just 'indeed' to 'only'. This word introduces the important statement that follows about Allah.
The alif at the end is written with a small vertical line (called an alif maqsurah) when it comes at the end of certain words. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be worshipped, to be taken as a deity

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to worship' into the noun 'a god'
The small alif above makes the 'aa' sound longer

steps.m: your (all of you) god (subject of this sentence)
steps.n: Added كُمُ meaning 'your (plural)' with ُ showing this is the subject of the sentence
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.
This is the basic demonstrative form that points to something
Adding ال with shadda changes this from pointing at something to connecting parts of a sentence. Here it connects back to 'your god' (إِلٰهُكُمُ) in the ayah.
This is the basic negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
The alif is elongated with a madda mark to show emphasis. This word negates إِلٰهَ (deity) in the phrase 'there is no deity except Him'
Connected the root letters that mean worship or deification
Changes verb 'to worship' into noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Changed to show there is 'no god' - the line over the alif and mark at the end show this word is being negated by لَا
This is a basic exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before it. It does not come from a root.

steps.m: except (excludes هُوَ from what came before)
steps.n: The shadda and alif are added to complete the exception particle. In this ayah, it excludes 'He (Allah)' from anything that came before.
This is a personal pronoun that refers to Allah in this ayah. It's the subject (doer) of the verb وَسِعَ (encompassed/extended) that comes after it.

steps.m: to encompass, contain, be vast enough for
he encompassed (subject of the relative clause after الَّذِي)
steps.n: Basic past tense form showing completed action. This word describes Allah's encompassing knowledge in this ayah.
Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to make it mean 'all' or 'every'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb وَسِعَ (encompassed)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'thing'
Added genitive case ending to show 'of a thing'
Changed the root from an action (to know) into the thing itself (knowledge), like changing 'write' to 'writing'

steps.m: knowledge (as the object being encompassed by وَسِعَ)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows that this knowledge is what is being encompassed in the phrase وَسِعَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ (He encompasses all things)
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذٰ (this) to specify what is being compared to
Added لِ to emphasize the comparison

steps.m: like that, in this way (referring to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to point back to what was mentioned before. This word introduces the next statement about telling stories of the past.

steps.m: to tell a story or news in order

steps.n: Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it 'he told a story' in the past tense
Changed from past tense (he told) to present tense (tell) by changing vowels and doubling middle letter
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word connects to عَلَيْكَ (to you) in the ayah.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'. It comes from the idea of being high or above something.
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to a pronoun. This is just a spelling change to help with pronunciation.

steps.m: upon you (object of نَقُصُّ - we narrate)
steps.n: Added the pronoun كَ meaning 'you' to show who is being acted upon. This word connects to نَقُصُّ (we narrate) to show what is being narrated upon.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what something is made of. Here it connects to the word أَنۢبَآءِ (news/stories) to show we are telling some of the previous stories.
Root meaning 'news' becomes a single piece of news by adding the vowels
Changes from single news to multiple news by following the special plural pattern أفعال

steps.m: of the news (multiple pieces) (part of مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ - from the news of)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this word is connected to the word مِنْ (from) that comes before it in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun connecting to سَبَقَ (passed/preceded) in this ayah. It introduces information about past events.
This is a special word that shows something definitely happened in the past. Here it emphasizes the verb سَبَقَ (passed/preceded).

steps.m: to go ahead or come before something else

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word refers to something that happened before 'ما' (what) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. قَدْ is a particle that adds emphasis or shows something is complete.
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what comes after it (ءَاتَيْنٰكَ meaning 'We have given you')
Changed the root into Form IV verb pattern to show causing someone to receive something
Added نا to show 'we' are doing the action
Added ك at the end to show you are receiving the action

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects the stories being told (أَنۢبَآءِ) to the act of narrating (نَقُصُّ), showing which stories are being told - specifically the ones from the past.
This word is usually used to mean 'from the presence of'
The vowels help show this word means 'from' or 'from the presence of'
Adding shadda on first letter adds emphasis
Added نَّا at the end to show this is from Allah's presence
Changed the action into a thing by adding ِ and ْ marks. Like how 'remind' becomes 'reminder' in English.
Added ًا to show this is what was given. In this ayah, Allah is saying He gave 'a reminder' to the Prophet.
This is a word that points to people, like saying 'who' or 'whoever' in English. It's used to start talking about someone without naming them specifically.
Added a sukun (ْ) mark to show where to stop when reading the word.
Added a shadda (ّ) because the word before it ends in a silent letter. In this ayah, this word is talking about the person who does the action 'turned away' (أَعْرَضَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he turned away (from عَنْهُ - from it)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning and changed the first vowel to sukun (ْ). This changes the meaning to deliberately turning away or avoiding something. In this ayah, it's followed by عَنْهُ which tells us what is being turned away from.
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance from something

steps.m: away from him (acted on by أَعْرَضَ - turned away)
steps.n: The هُ at the end means 'him'. This word shows what is being turned away from in the phrase 'whoever turns away from him'.
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger and more certain

steps.m: so/then indeed (connects to أَعْرَضَ - turned away)
steps.n: The فَ connects this to what happened before - because someone turned away, then this result happens

steps.m: so/then indeed he (subject of يَحْمِلُ - carries)
steps.n: The هُ means 'he/him' and connects to the verb يَحْمِلُ (carries) that comes after it. The ۥ mark shows you stretch the sound a little when reading.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, kasra on middle root letter, and damma at end. This verb describes what someone will do on the Day of Judgment (يَوْمَ)
The damma at the end marks this as a present tense indicative verb
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. The و becomes silent with sukun.
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of the verb يَحْمِلُ (he carries/bears) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the action itself. The و changes to ي because it comes after kasra
Added ة to make it refer to the specific event of resurrection
Added ٱلْ to specify this is THE resurrection
Added kasra at the end to show it belongs to 'day', with a small spelling change in the middle
Changed from a root meaning 'carry load' into a noun meaning the actual 'load/burden' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: a burden (object of يَحْمِلُ - he carries)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is the thing being carried in the phrase 'he carries a burden'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Adding alif after first letter and kasra after second letter makes it describe someone who does the action
The long alif adds emphasis to show something is permanent or intense

steps.m: those who stay permanently (describing their state in فِيهِ - in it)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it plural and shows it's describing their state in the next word فِيهِ (in it)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment

steps.m: in it (referring to remaining/staying mentioned in خٰلِدِينَ)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what or who is being referred to. This connects back to the state of remaining/staying mentioned in the word خٰلِدِينَ before it.
The root letters changed to make a past tense verb meaning 'it became bad' - the middle letter و turned into ا
Added an alif after س to show the long 'aa' sound

steps.m: and it became bad (describing حِمْلًا - their burden)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the start to mean 'and'. This word describes how bad their burden will be on the Day of Judgment.
This is a basic preposition showing direction, purpose, or possession
Added the third person masculine plural pronoun suffix هُمْ to show who the preposition is directed to
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to form the basic noun. This word refers to the time when something happens.

steps.m: day (object of سَآءَ - it was bad)
steps.n: Added fatḥa at the end to show this word is receiving the action. It tells us when something was bad.

steps.m: standing up, rising up (as a concept)
steps.n: The root letters change to make a noun meaning 'the act of standing' - the middle letter و becomes ي
ة transforms the verbal noun into a noun indicating a specific instance
Definite article prefix makes the noun definite. This word describes يَوْمَ (Day) in the ayah.

steps.m: the resurrection (possessed by/connected to يَوْمَ - Day)
steps.n: Genitive case marker ِ shows this word is possessed by or connected to يَوْمَ (Day) in the ayah
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the thing that is carried or borne

steps.m: a burden (as something being carried on the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being carried, connecting to the context of the ayah about the Day of Judgment
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to form the basic noun

steps.m: on the day (when يُنفَخُ - it is blown)
steps.n: The ending fatha shows this word tells us when something happens. It's connected to the verb يُنفَخُ (it is blown) that comes after it.

steps.m: to blow air or breathe into

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: it is blown (into الصُّورِ - the horn)
steps.n: Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start and ُ at end. The horn (الصُّورِ) is what's being blown into.
Basic preposition showing location or position
The ى extends the meaning to show movement into something. Here it specifically shows the blowing going into the horn.
Added damma to create noun meaning the instrument itself

steps.m: the horn (that is blown into يُنفَخُ فِى)
steps.n: The word 'horn' became 'the horn' by adding ال (the). The ص gets doubled (shadda) when ال is added. This is the special horn that will be blown to signal Judgment Day.

steps.m: to gather or collect together (especially for a purpose)
we gather (subject gathering ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ - the criminals)
steps.n: The نَ at the start shows 'we' are doing the action. In this ayah, we are gathering the criminals (ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ).

steps.m: and we gather (subject gathering ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ - the criminals)
steps.n: Adds و at the beginning to connect this action with the previous phrase about the trumpet being blown (يُنفَخُ فِى ٱلصُّورِ)

steps.m: to do wrong or commit a crime

ٱلْ makes it specific, pointing to particular wrongdoers. The َ at the end helps with pronunciation when connecting to the next word.

steps.m: blue-eyed ones (object of وَنَحْشُرُ/we will gather)

steps.m: if (introducing a condition in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic conditional word that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects with لَّبِثْتُمْ (you remained) to form the meaning 'if you remained'

steps.m: if (introducing a condition in this ayah)
steps.n: Added the kasra (ِ) under the إ to show the correct pronunciation. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to stay somewhere for a period

This is a standalone pronoun that means 'we'. Added vowel markings to show it's complete.
Added a shadda (double letter mark) to emphasize the word, making it stronger like saying 'WE' instead of just 'we'.

Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form

Adds ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action

Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he stayed'
Added emphasis to the beginning of the word

Added the pronoun ه with kasra (هِ) at the end to show this means 'in it', referring back to the Quran mentioned earlier in the ayah
This is a basic preposition showing where something comes from or a part of something. It needs to connect to the word after it.
Added a fatha (َ) at the end to make it easier to pronounce when connecting to the next word ٱلْوَعِيدِ (the warning).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The ي between ع and د changes it from an action (to warn) into the warning itself
Added ال to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to what comes before it
This is a basic word that expresses hope or expectation that something will happen. It's like saying 'hopefully' or 'maybe' in English, but with more optimism.

steps.m: perhaps they (expressing hope that they will يَتَّقُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who we're hoping about (they/them). This connects to the next word يَتَّقُونَ (they guard against evil) - we're expressing hope that they will do this action.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ت with shadda to show doing action to oneself
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word follows لَعَلَّهُمْ (perhaps they) in the ayah.
This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between two options. In this ayah, it connects 'they become mindful' with 'it causes them to remember'.
Added the vowel mark fatha (َ) on أ and the sukun (ْ) on و to show correct pronunciation
Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by adding the vowels a-a-a
Added alif at beginning to show causative meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added هُ to show this is about him/it
Added م with sukun to change 'him' to 'them'

steps.m: remembrance (the act or result of remembering)
steps.n: Changed the action into a thing by adding ِ and ْ marks. Like how 'remember' becomes 'remembrance' in English.

steps.m: a remembrance (object of أَنزَلْنٰهُ - We sent it down)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this is what was sent down (like saying 'We sent down a remembrance'). This connects to the verb أَنزَلْنٰهُ (We sent it down) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ى at end to show doing something to make oneself higher/greater

steps.m: So He rose up/exalted Himself (subject of ٱللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ (meaning 'so/then') at the start to connect with what follows. The word shows Allah rising above or being higher than something.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
Added damma to show this is the subject
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Changes from verb 'to rule' into noun meaning 'one who rules'
Added ال to make it specific
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: to be true, real, or right

steps.n: Root letters ح-ق-ق became a noun حَقّ by adding fatha and combining the double ق with shadda
Added ال to show we're talking about absolute truth. This word is what is being described as exalted in فَتَعٰلَى (so He is exalted)
Added ُ to show this word is the subject being described as exalted
This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَعْجَلْ (hasten) into 'do not hasten'
Added وَ to connect this command with what came before in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' and changed vowels to sukun for jussive mood because it's a negative command with لا. This makes it mean 'do not hurry' in the context.

steps.m: to read or recite (especially out loud)
that which is meant to be read/recited repeatedly
steps.n: Changes the verb into a special noun that shows something meant to be read or recited often. The ء is added to separate the two vowels.

steps.m: the Quran (the specific book of recitation)
steps.n: Adds ٱلْ to show we're talking about the specific Quran, not just any recitation
Adds ِ to show the Quran is being affected by something (in this case, by the word بِ that will come next)

steps.m: with/by the Quran (connected to تَعْجَلْ - do not hasten)
steps.n: Adds بِ meaning 'with' or 'by' at the start. In this ayah, it connects to the word تَعْجَلْ (do not hasten), showing not to hasten with/regarding the Quran.
This is a preposition that shows where something starts from or comes from. Here it connects to the word قَبْلِ (before) to show 'from before'.
Added fatha to first letter and sukun to middle letter to create the noun meaning 'before'

steps.m: before (of أَن يُقْضَىٰٓ - it being completed)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word comes after مِن (from/before) in the ayah
This is a particle that introduces a new clause. Here it connects to the verb يُقْضَىٰٓ (is completed) to show what not to be hasty about.

steps.m: to complete, finish, or make final

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make past tense. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: it is being completed (before إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُۥ)
steps.n: Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show this is happening to something (passive voice) rather than someone doing it. This word connects to إِلَيْكَ وَحْيُهُۥ (its revelation to you) in the ayah.
Added ى at the end to complete the verb form since this word comes from a root ending in ي
This is the basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.
The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that happens before pronouns.
Added the pronoun ك at the end to show who is being addressed - in this case, 'you' (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this ayah)

steps.m: to inspire or communicate something directly to someone's heart/mind

steps.n: Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject of يُقْضَىٰٓ (is completed)
The word 'revelation' got the attached pronoun 'his' (هُ) at the end, making it 'his revelation'. It's still doing the action in the sentence.
Changed to a command by making it short and adding a 'u' sound on top. It's telling someone to say رَّبِّ (my Lord) that comes next.
Adding وَ connects this command to the previous statement about not rushing with the Quran

steps.m: Lord (one who nurtures and takes care of everything)
steps.n: Connected letters with fatha and doubled middle letter to show intensity of nurturing quality
Added kasra at end to show this is being used to call upon or address Allah directly

steps.m: my Lord! (addressing Allah directly with emphasis)
steps.n: Added shadda on first letter to emphasize the direct address to Allah

steps.m: to increase or add to something

steps.n: The root letters are made into a command by dropping the middle letter ي and adding a kasra (i) sound at the start

steps.m: increase for me! (command to increase knowledge for me)
steps.n: Added نِى at the end to show who the increase is for (me). This word is connected to عِلْمًا (knowledge) in the ayah, showing what should be increased.
Changed the action of knowing into the thing called knowledge, like changing 'know' to 'knowledge' in English
Added ًا at the end to show this is what we want more of - it's the thing being increased in the phrase 'increase me in knowledge'
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Added لَ at the beginning to add extra emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'surely'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to promise or make an agreement

steps.n: Adding the basic vowel pattern for past tense verbs
Changed the last vowel to sukun to prepare for adding 'we'
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did this action
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows direction or destination
Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is a writing variation that doesn't change the meaning
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show the sound is stretched slightly when reading
Changed the root into the proper name Adam by adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle and a fatha at the end. This name is historically connected to the word's root meaning of dark/brown color.
Basic preposition showing where or when something starts. In this ayah, it connects the verb عَهِدْنَآ (We made a covenant) with قَبْلُ (before) to show when the covenant was made.

steps.m: to be in front of something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to turn the concept of 'being in front' into a word meaning 'front' or 'before'
Added ُ at the end to show this word means 'before' as a time reference, connecting to Adam's name that comes after it
Basic past tense verb showing someone forgot something

steps.m: and so he forgot (after عَهِدْنَآ - We took a covenant)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened as a result of something before it. In this ayah, it shows Adam forgot after Allah took a covenant with him.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' in the past
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Adds نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. The word is negated by لَمْ which comes before it, making it mean 'we did not find'
Added sukun at the end because it comes after لَمْ which requires the verb to end with sukun
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: for him (connected to فَنَسِىَ - then he forgot)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who the preposition is directed to. This connects to the previous verb فَنَسِىَ (then he forgot) to show what was forgotten.
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Changed from a root meaning 'to be determined' into a noun meaning 'determination' by adding vowel marks (fatHa and sukun)

steps.m: determination (object of نَجِدْ - we did not find)
steps.n: Added -an ending to show this is the thing that was not found
This is a word that points to a time in the past, like saying "Remember when..." in English. It introduces the story that follows about the angels and Adam.
The و connects this story to previous stories, showing it's part of a series of reminders about past events
The middle letter و drops out and is replaced with a sukun (ْ) on ل. This makes the basic command form.
Adding نا at the end changes it from 'say!' to 'we said'
Changed the root 'to control' into 'angel' by adding the vowels a-a (malak)
Added alif to extend the word following a common pattern for important beings
The word for 'angel' changes its middle letters to make it plural, like how we say 'mouse' becomes 'mice'
Added feminine ending to complete the plural form
Added definite article to specify these particular angels

steps.m: to the angels (who were commanded اسْجُدُوا۟)
steps.n: Added لِ (li) at the start to mean 'to/for' the angels
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Removed first vowel and changed middle vowel to 'u' to make it a command to one person
Added ٱ at the start - this is how commands/orders start in Arabic
Added وا at the end to show this command is for a group. This command was given to the angels to prostrate to Adam.
This is the name of the first human
The name Adam comes from root meaning brown/dark-skinned
Added a mark (fatha) at the end to show Adam is the one being prostrated to

steps.m: to Adam (showing who they should prostrate to)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show direction - who they should prostrate to

steps.m: to prostrate, to bow down completely in submission
he prostrated (in response to أَبَىٰ - he refused)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb. This word contrasts with أَبَىٰ (he refused) in the ayah.

steps.m: they prostrated (referring to الْمَلٰٓئِكَةِ - the angels)
steps.n: Changed from singular to plural by adding وا to show multiple angels prostrating
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of something before it
This is a basic particle used to show that something is being excluded or is different from what came before it. It's like saying 'but not' in English.
Added ا at the end to make it mean 'except' - this word always needs something after it to show what is being excluded
The ا is written as آ to show it's pronounced longer. This connects to the next word إِبْلِيسَ (Iblis) to show he was the exception to the angels who prostrated.
This is a proper name borrowed into Arabic, possibly from Greek 'diabolos'. It became Satan's name after he refused to bow to Adam.

steps.m: Iblis (as the subject being excepted from سَجَدُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because Iblis is being excepted (excluded) from the group who prostrated in the phrase 'they prostrated except Iblis'
Changed to past tense 'he refused' by adding fathas, ending with ى instead of ي
Added alif maqsurah (ىٰ) which is just a spelling variation of ى at the end of words
Changed from basic root 'to say' into a short command meaning 'say!' - like telling someone to speak
Adding نَا changes it from a command to past tense and shows we did the action

steps.m: then we said (introducing what was said to آدَمُ)
steps.n: Adding فَ shows this follows from previous events. This word introduces what was said to Adam, as shown in the rest of the ayah.
Root letters أ-د-م became the name Adam (آدَم) by adding a long 'aa' sound at start
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject being addressed

steps.m: O Adam (being addressed by فَقُلْنَا - so We said)
steps.n: Added يٰٓ at beginning to show this is calling out to Adam. This word is being directly addressed as shown by فَقُلْنَا (so We said) before it.
This is the basic form of the particle that connects sentences

steps.m: indeed/certainly (introduces what Allah said about عَدُوٌّ)
steps.n: Adding shadda to ن changes this from a conditional particle to an emphasis particle. It makes what comes after it stronger and more certain.
This is the basic attention-drawing part that starts many pointing words
Adding ذ changes it from just getting attention to actually pointing at something
The ا at the end shows we're pointing to a masculine thing. In this ayah, it's pointing to عَدُوٌّ (enemy)

steps.m: to be hostile, to transgress against

steps.n: Changes from verb 'being hostile' to noun 'enemy' by adding specific vowel pattern
Added shadda and tanween to show this is describing an undefined enemy. This word is describing the relationship to Adam mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Basic preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Adding shadda emphasizes the meaning to show certainty
Adding كَ shows this is directed to a single male person being spoken to
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'spouse'
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is for or to someone/something
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to 'you'
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous phrase
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يُخْرِجَنَّكُمَا (he expels you both) into its opposite meaning
The فَ shows this is a result or consequence of what came before. It connects to the previous statement about the enemy.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changed to causative form - making something happen rather than doing it directly

steps.m: he will surely make (something/someone) go out
steps.n: Added emphasis suffix to show certainty

steps.m: he will surely expel you both
steps.n: Added dual pronoun suffix to show who is being expelled
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word الْجَنَّةِ (Paradise). This combination means 'from Paradise'

steps.m: to cover or hide (like how a garden's trees cover the ground)

steps.n: The doubled ن makes the hiding/covering more complete or intense
Added ة to make it refer to a specific place with covering (garden)
Added ال to show we're talking about a specific garden
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word مِنَ (from/of) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels, weak letter و changes to ى at end
Added ت at start to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense

steps.m: and thus you will struggle/suffer (consequence of being expelled from Paradise)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this is a result of the previous action (being expelled), and added alif at end to show emphasis
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show this is the basic emphasis word

steps.m: indeed/verily (strongly emphasizes لَكَ 'for you' that follows)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to make the emphasis stronger. This word connects to لَكَ (for you) in the next part of the ayah to strongly emphasize what Allah is telling Adam.
Added fatha to show how the word is pronounced

steps.m: for you (speaking to one male) (connected to تَجُوعَ - you will be hungry)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this is directed to one male person. This word connects to تَجُوعَ (you will be hungry) later in the ayah, showing what won't happen to the person being spoken to.
Connected the two separate words أَنْ (that) and لَا (not)

steps.m: that not (introduces something that won't happen to تَجُوعَ)
steps.n: The نْ sound blends into the ل creating a stronger ل sound (shown by ّ). This word introduces what won't happen - in this case, being hungry (تَجُوعَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was hungry' (past) to 'you are hungry' (present) by changing the first vowel

steps.m: that you become hungry (connected to أَلَّا meaning 'that not')
steps.n: Added تَ prefix to show 'you' as the subject. The word follows أَلَّا (that not) in the ayah, making it mean 'that you will not be hungry'
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Since this is a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
Added ها to show this refers to Paradise (الجنة) which was mentioned earlier in the story. الجنة is feminine in Arabic, so we use the feminine pronoun.
Starting with the basic negative particle لا

steps.m: and not (connects to تَجُوعَ - you will hunger)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative statement with the previous phrase, showing another thing that won't happen
This root carries the basic meaning of being without covering or exposed
Basic root letters got vowels added to make a past tense verb meaning 'he became bare'
Added تَ at start to show 'you' are doing the action. The ي changed to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the final ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
This is a basic particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it
The و at the beginning connects this with the previous statement

steps.m: and indeed you (subject of لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows this is talking to one male person. This 'you' is the subject who 'will not thirst' (لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟) in the following phrase.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it not happen. Here it tells us you will NOT be thirsty.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added vowels to show a state or feeling
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' and changed vowels for present tense

steps.m: you all become thirsty (in relation to لَا before it meaning 'not')
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people, and hamza changed to ؤ because it comes after damma
This is a basic word that shows location or being inside something. It's one of the most common Arabic words.
Added هَا ('ha') at the end to mean 'in it', where 'it' refers back to Paradise. Like adding 'it' to make 'in' become 'in it'
This is the basic negative word that negates the verb تَضْحَىٰ (suffer from sun's heat) in this ayah
The و connects this negative with the previous statement about not being thirsty (لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟), showing these are two things that won't happen in Paradise
ت at start shows this is happening to 'you'

steps.m: you suffer from sun exposure (in relation to لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟ - you will not thirst)
steps.n: Changed final و to ى with alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) due to weak letter rules. This word is connected to لَا تَظْمَؤُا۟ (you will not thirst) in describing the comforts of Paradise.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word is connected to قَالَ (he said) in the next part of the ayah.

steps.m: and then he whispered secretly (subject of قَالَ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else
This is a preposition showing direction. In this ayah, it connects to الشَّيْطٰنُ (Satan) who is doing the whispering
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change
The هِ suffix shows this means 'to him', referring back to Adam who is being whispered to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added ي and ا to create intensive active participle form showing someone who embodies this quality
Added definite article ال which causes shadda on ش. This specifies we're talking about Satan specifically.
Added damma to show this is the subject doing the whispering in the verse. The alif is written as a long ā.
Added fatha vowels to make past tense. This word introduces what the devil said to Adam.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs

steps.m: to be brown/dark-skinned, to mix with soil

steps.n: Root letters turned into the name Adam by adding a long 'aa' sound at the start
Added damma to show Adam is the subject doing the action
Added يٰ at beginning to show Adam is being called or addressed directly

steps.m: do you want to know if
steps.n: This is a question word that starts yes/no questions. In this ayah, Satan is asking Adam if he wants to know about something.

steps.m: do you want to know if (introducing أَدُلُّكَ - shall I guide you)
steps.n: Shows how to say 'hal' - ha with a short 'a' sound and a silent l. Used here to ask 'would you like' before offering guidance.

steps.m: to point to, to show, to guide

steps.n: When two same letters come together, they combine with a shadda
Changes from 'he' to 'I' by adding أ at the start
Adds second person masculine singular object pronoun
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else. Here it connects to the word شَجَرَةِ (tree) to show what Adam is being guided to.
Added vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - 'a' sound on ع and 'a' sound on ل
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it should be pronounced with a long 'aa' sound at the end
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun for trees as a group
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific tree instead of trees in general
Added kasra because it follows عَلَىٰ (upon) in the phrase عَلَىٰ شَجَرَةِ (upon a tree)
Changed from basic root meaning 'last forever' to a noun meaning 'eternal life' by adding the vowels u and sukoon
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the' in English. In this ayah, it's talking about a specific type of eternal life that Satan was tempting Adam with.
Added ِ to show this word is connected to شَجَرَةِ (tree) - it's the 'tree of eternal life'
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the place or concept of rule/authority

steps.m: and a kingdom (offered to آدَمُ by الشَّيْطٰنُ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this to what came before (eternal life) in Satan's temptation of Adam
This is a basic negative word that makes the verb يَبْلَىٰ (perish/decay) into 'does not perish/decay'
The shadda (ّ) makes the negation stronger, like saying 'absolutely not' or 'not at all' instead of just 'not'
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'a' between letters
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The و changes to ى at the end because it's a weak verb.
Added superscript alif (ٰ) to indicate the long vowel sound. This word describes مُلْكٍ (kingdom/dominion) that will never wear out or perish.
Connected the root letters with fathas to make the past tense verb
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happened after something else

steps.m: and then they both ate (referring to Adam and his wife)
steps.n: Added alif at the end to show two people (Adam and his wife) did the action together
This is a combination of مِنْ (from) and ها (it/her)
Added vowel marks (fatha) between the letters and changed the final و to ا to make it past tense 'it became apparent'
Added feminine marker ت to show the subject is feminine (their private parts/سَوْءٰتُهُمَا)

steps.m: and then it (feminine) became apparent (subject of سَوْءٰتُهُمَا)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before (them eating from it)
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added هُمَا which means 'both of them' - this is used when referring to exactly two people

steps.m: a private part (something to be hidden)
steps.n: Forms a noun for one specific instance of something shameful or meant to be hidden
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple private parts

steps.m: their (dual) private parts (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Adds possession for two people (Adam and Eve) and changes the final vowel
Connected the root letters with fatha-kasra voweling to make past tense. This verb introduces what Adam and Eve began doing (covering themselves with leaves)
Added alif to show two people (Adam and Eve) doing the action together
Added و to connect this to the previous action (فَبَدَتْ - became apparent) in the story sequence
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end

steps.m: they both patch/cover (referring to Adam and Eve)
steps.n: Added انِ to show two people (Adam and Eve) doing the action together
The ى at the end changes to ي with a sukun when connecting to pronouns
Added هِمَا at the end to show this applies to exactly two people
Basic preposition showing something comes from somewhere. In this ayah, it shows that they ate from the leaves.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic noun
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to 'the garden' (الجَنَّةِ) that comes after it in the ayah
Adding shadda makes the action stronger and more intense
Added ة to make it a specific place that is covered/protected
Added ال to make it refer to a specific garden
Added kasra to show this connects to the words before it (مِن وَرَقِ - from leaves of)
Changed from basic root meaning 'disobey' to past tense 'he disobeyed' by adding fathas and changing final ي to ا
Adding conjunction و with fatha to connect to previous phrase
Changed alif to ى with an overextension mark (ٰ) which is a common spelling variation
Added a small alif (ٓ) above as a pronunciation guide
Root letters turned into the name Adam by adding a long 'aa' sound at the start

steps.m: Adam (as the subject of عَصَىٰ - disobeyed)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show Adam is doing the action in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows intensity, indicating complete mastery and lordship
Adding the possessive pronoun changes it from 'Lord' to 'his Lord'. This word is the direct object (what is being acted upon) of the verb عصى (disobeyed).

steps.m: to stray from the right path

steps.n: Added fathas to make past tense. This shows what Adam did after he disobeyed (عَصَىٰٓ) in the ayah.
Added فَ to show this happened as a result of the disobedience mentioned before
Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) which is just a writing style for the alif at the end
This is a connecting word that shows sequence of events. It's stronger than just 'and' - it shows a clear order of events.
Added the vowel marks ُ (u-sound) and ّ (doubled m-sound) to complete the word. This word shows that after something happened before, Allah chose Adam.
Root letters connected with fathas (a-sounds) to make past tense 'he' verb
Added اِ at start and ت in middle to show someone carefully choosing something special for themselves
Added هُ at the end to show who was chosen

steps.m: He chose him specially (subject of رَبُّهُۥ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show who was chosen. This word connects to رَبُّهُۥ (his Lord) who is doing the choosing.

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and help grow

steps.n: Doubling the ب makes it stronger - shows complete mastery and care
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this Lord is doing something - specifically choosing/selecting (اجتبى) Adam in this verse
Adding هُ (hu) at the end means 'his', showing this is Adam's Lord who chose him
The root letters were joined together and vowels added to make a past tense verb. The middle letter و became ا, which is common for this type of word.

steps.m: so he turned back in repentance (after اجْتَبٰهُ - He chose him)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this action follows after Allah chose him (اجْتَبٰهُ). The فَ here shows the sequence of events.
This is the basic preposition showing direction or focus towards something
The ى changes to ي with sukun to connect with the coming pronoun

steps.m: upon him, towards him (referring to آدَمَ/Adam)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show this is happening to 'him' (referring back to Adam in this ayah)

steps.m: to guide or show the right path
he guided (after فَتَابَ - he turned in repentance)
steps.n: Added fathas to show past tense. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end. This action follows after Allah accepted Adam's repentance.
Added و (meaning 'and') to connect this action with what happened before
Added alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound more clearly
Changed root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels a-a-a. Used to show Allah said something.
The و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in hollow verbs.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form. This word follows after قَالَ (he said) in the ayah.
Changed from past tense 'he went down' to command form 'go down!'

steps.m: you two go down! (command for two people)
steps.n: Adding ا makes it a command for two people instead of one

steps.m: you two go down! (command for two people)
steps.n: Adding ٱ at start helps pronunciation of the command
Combination of preposition مِنْ (from) and feminine pronoun هَا (it/her)

steps.m: to join or bring things together

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'gathered' to adjective meaning 'all'

steps.m: all together (describing how they should go down)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the action happens. This connects to the command 'go down' (ٱهْبِطَا) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word is the subject of عَدُوٌّ (enemy) in the phrase 'some are enemies'.
Added ُ to show this is doing the action (subject) and كُمْ meaning 'you all/your'. Together they mean 'some of you' as the subject of 'are enemies'.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the noun meaning 'some' or 'part'. This word is part of the phrase 'some of you to others' in the ayah.
Adds the preposition لِ to indicate 'to' and tanwin kasra to make the noun indefinite

steps.m: to be hostile, to transgress against

steps.n: Added vowels and doubled the final و to make it stronger, showing someone who is actively hostile
Added double damma to show this is describing what some of you will be to others (بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ = some of you will be enemies to others)
Adding ما to إِنْ makes the condition more general, changing from a specific 'if' to a broader 'whenever'. The ن and م merge with a shadda.
Adding فَ at the beginning shows this follows from something else. Here it connects to the guidance (هُدًى) mentioned later in the verse.

steps.m: he comes (imperfect verb in فَإِمَّا...conditional statement)
steps.n: Adds يَ at beginning to show ongoing/future action. This verb is part of a conditional statement starting with فَإِمَّا (then if/whenever)
Adds heavy emphasis نَّ to show something will definitely happen
Added كُم at the end to show who receives the action (you all). Allah is speaking to Adam and Eve about guidance that will come to their descendants.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added ي ('me') and doubled the ن to connect it smoothly
The ن gets a shadda for emphasis, and the ي is written as ى at the end of words
Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning the concept itself rather than the action
Added tanween and alif maqsura to make it a complete abstract noun meaning 'guidance'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it (اتَّبَعَ - follows)
Added فَ at the beginning to show this starts a new part of the sentence that follows from what was said before. This word is the subject (doer) of the verb اتَّبَعَ (follows) in this ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added Form VIII pattern which shows doing action for oneself
Doubling the ت shows doing the action strongly or repeatedly
The initial اِ is written as ٱ in the Quran

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changes from active (guiding) to passive (being guided)
Added ا and َ to show this is 'my guidance'. This connects it to the speaker (Allah in this case).
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. This word negates the verb يَضِلُّ (go astray) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form

steps.m: he loses/is losing the right way
steps.n: Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and combined the double ل into ّ

steps.m: he loses/is losing the right way (subject of فَلَا)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the main subject of the sentence. This word comes after فَلَا (then not) in the ayah.
Added fatha to show this is the basic negative particle لَا that negates the verb يَشْقَىٰ (become miserable) that comes after it
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, showing this is another result that follows from following guidance
Basic past tense pattern for verbs showing states/feelings
Added يَ prefix for present tense and changed vowels for present tense pattern. The weak letter changes form in present tense.
Added alif khanjariyah (small alif) to indicate the long vowel sound
This is a special word used to refer to any person in general. Here it introduces the person who will turn away (أَعْرَضَ) from Allah's reminder.

steps.m: and whoever (subject of أَعْرَضَ - turned away)
steps.n: The و at the beginning means 'and' - it connects this statement about whoever turns away to what was mentioned before. This introduces a new scenario or consequence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he deliberately turned away (from ذِكْرِى - My remembrance)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to show this is done deliberately or with intensity. This verb connects to 'My remembrance' (ذِكْرِى) in the ayah using عَن (from/away from).
This is a basic preposition showing separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it connects with ذِكْرِى (My remembrance) to show turning away from Allah's remembrance.
Added fatha mark to show correct pronunciation
Changed from basic root meaning 'remember' into the noun 'remembrance' by adding the vowel marks i and sukoon

steps.m: my remembrance (object of أَعْرَضَ عَن - turned away from)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show it belongs to me. This word is what is being turned away from in the phrase 'turned away from my remembrance'
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show this emphasis.

steps.m: then indeed (connects to وَمَنْ أَعْرَضَ - and whoever turns away)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a result of what was mentioned before. Here it connects to 'whoever turns away' to show what will happen as a result.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound on the preposition ل
Added هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to
Added the prolongation mark ۥ to show the extended pronunciation of the final vowel. This word connects to مَعِيشَةً (life/livelihood) in the ayah, showing whose life is being described.
Added مَ at the beginning to show this is about how or where something happens
Added ة to make it refer to one specific instance or type of living

steps.m: a way of life (as the object of لَهُ - he has)
steps.n: Added ً to show this is the object being possessed in the phrase 'he has a way of life'
Basic noun form showing the state of being tight/constricted
Added tanween to show this word describes the type of life (معيشة) mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to gather or collect together in one place

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he gathered' to 'we gather' by adding ن at start
Added damma at end to show this is present tense
Added هُ at the end to show who is being gathered (him)

steps.m: and we gather him (connected to أَعْرَضَ - turned away)
steps.n: Added وَ at beginning to connect this action with the previous one (turning away). The small ۥ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.
Added vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'a day'

steps.m: day (object of وَنَحْشُرُهُۥ - we will gather him)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of gathering. It answers the question 'When will we gather him?' - on that Day.
