The root letters change to make a noun meaning 'the act of standing' by adding a long 'ee' sound (ي) in the middle and 'aa' sound (ا) after it

steps.m: a single instance of rising/standing (specifically resurrection)
steps.n: The ة makes it refer to one specific type of rising - the resurrection on Judgment Day

steps.m: the resurrection (specifically the Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it refer to the specific, well-known resurrection on Judgment Day

steps.m: the resurrection (specifically the Day of Judgment, being described in this ayah)
steps.n: The ِ at the end shows this word is being described or connected to something else in the sentence
Added vowels (fatha, kasra, fatha) to make 'he became blind' in past tense
Changed to pattern أَفْعَل to describe someone who has this quality

steps.m: blind (in relation to يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيٰمَةِ - Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added the long alif mark (ٰ) to show this is the final form. In this ayah, it describes the state of being raised blind on the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: he said (introducing his words: رَبِّ لِمَ حَشَرْتَنِىٓ أَعْمَىٰ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show this happened in the past. This word introduces what he actually said next in the ayah.
This is just a spelling change - the meaning stays exactly the same
Connected the root letters with fatha to create basic past tense verb
Double the middle letter to show strong/intense nurturing

steps.m: O my Lord! (being called out to)
steps.n: The kasra here shows this is a vocative form - calling out to 'Lord'. This word follows قَالَ (he said) and starts what is being said.
This word combines لِ (for) and ما (what). Connected the parts together.

steps.m: why (asking about the reason he was raised blind)
steps.n: The ا is dropped at the end to make it shorter. This is a common way to ask 'why' in questions. In this ayah, it's being used by someone asking Allah why they were raised blind when they could see before.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at the end to show 'you' did the action, and changed the last vowel to sukun
Added نِي at the end to show the action was done to 'me'
Added elongation mark (ٓ) to show emphasis in questioning tone, as this word appears in a question asking 'Why have you gathered me blind?'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb (became blind) to an adjective (blind) by adding أَ at the start and changing the ending. Makes it stronger - like 'totally blind'
Added the small alif (ٰ) to show this is the final form. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it or shows something is complete
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: and indeed (while كُنتُ بَصِيرًا - I was seeing)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word connects to كُنتُ بَصِيرًا (I was seeing) to show contrast with the previous state.

steps.m: to be or exist in a state/condition

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Command form ('Be!') - the middle و is dropped and vowels change to make it shorter and more direct
Added تُ to show 'I' did this in the past. In this ayah, the person is saying they used to be able to see before being made blind.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed the pattern to show someone who has this quality strongly

steps.m: seeing/understanding clearly (as a state of كُنتُ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes a state. This word is describing how 'I was' (كُنتُ) in the ayah.
Added vowels to show this is about someone (he) doing the action in the past. This word introduces what he said in the next part.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter - this is a common change in Arabic verbs
Starting with the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Adding ٰلِ changes the meaning from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ at the end makes it more specific, like saying 'that specific thing'

steps.m: like that (referring to how the person ignored Allah's signs)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning changes it to mean 'like that' or 'similarly'. In this ayah, it's comparing how the person will be forgotten just like they forgot Allah's signs.
Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Added ت to show it's 'she' and removed the weak letter ي which always drops out in this pattern

steps.m: she came to you (referring to the signs/verses mentioned in ءَايٰتُنَا)
steps.n: Added كَ to show who was being come to. This connects to the next word ءَايٰتُنَا (our signs/verses) which explains what came.
Makes the word plural by adding the feminine plural ending
Signs/miracles - plural form of آية (ayah)
Added 'our' and nominative case marker. This word is the subject doing the action in أَتَتْ (came to you).
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The ي is a weak letter that stays as is.
Changed subject to 'you' (masculine singular) by adding تَ at the end. This matches how Allah is addressing someone directly in this ayah.

steps.m: you forgot/neglected it (referring to Allah's signs mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what was forgotten - referring back to آيَاتُنَا (Our signs) mentioned in the previous ayah

steps.m: so you forgot/neglected it (referring to Allah's signs)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this is a consequence - because you forgot Allah's signs before, so today you will be forgotten (connecting to تُنسَىٰ in the next ayah)
This is the base demonstrative particle meaning 'this'. The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this form.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' to 'that', making it point to something farther away
Adding كَ at the end points to something far away, meaning 'that' (like pointing at something)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Added vowels to make the basic noun form. This word refers to a single day or period of time.
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific and َ at the end to show it answers 'when?'. This word connects to تُنسَىٰ (you will be forgotten) by showing when that will happen.
Basic past tense verb meaning 'forgot'
Changed from 'he forgot' to 'you are forgotten' by adding تُ at start and changing inside vowels to make it passive. The ى at the end shows it's happening to someone.
Added dagger alif (ٰ) after ى to show the long 'aa' sound clearly
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'
The alif is elongated to show distance, and kasra is added to connect to next particle
The كَ makes the demonstrative more specific and emphatic
The و connects this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: we repay (those who أَسْرَفَ - exceeded limits)
steps.n: Added ن at start to show 'we', changed vowels to show present tense. This verb shows what Allah will do to those who exceeded limits mentioned later in the ayah.

steps.m: we repay (those who أَسْرَفَ - exceeded limits)
steps.n: Changed the final ي to ى which is just a writing style variation with no meaning change
This is a relative pronoun that introduces a description of a type of person. Here it connects to the verb أَسْرَفَ (transgressed/exceeded bounds) to describe 'whoever transgressed'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the first vowel to sukun (ْ) to make the meaning stronger. This word describes someone who didn't believe in the signs of their Lord.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُؤْمِنْ) mean 'did not' instead of 'does not'
The و at the beginning connects this with what came before it, showing these are related thoughts

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Adding vowel marks creates the basic past tense verb form
Added a long 'aa' sound at start to show stronger/more intense belief

steps.m: he believes (in آيٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ - signs of his Lord)
steps.n: Changed to present tense with يُ prefix. The hamza gets sukun and middle letter gets kasra. This word connects to 'signs of his Lord' in the ayah.

steps.m: he believes (in آيٰتِ رَبِّهِۦ - signs of his Lord)
steps.n: The vowel mark at the end changes to a small circle (sukun) because of the next word

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be evident

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
Added ات to make it plural (more than one sign)
Added بِ at the beginning to mean 'by/with' and changed the ending to ِ to show it's connected to the word رَبّ (Lord) that comes after it

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this describes someone who nurtures and cares for others perfectly and completely
The kasra (ِ) shows this word belongs to something else, making it mean 'of Lord' or 'Lord's'
Adding هِ shows the Lord belongs to 'him' (referring back to whoever didn't believe). The whole phrase ءَايٰتِ رَبِّهِ means 'signs of his Lord'
The ۦ after هِ shows you should stretch the sound a bit when reading. The meaning stays the same as رَبِّهِ.
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the result of the action.
Adding و at the beginning connects this word to what came before it
Adding لَ adds emphasis to show this is definitely true

steps.m: and surely the punishment (subject of أَشَدُّ)
steps.n: Adding ُ shows this word is the subject doing the action in أَشَدُّ (is more severe)

steps.m: to come after, to be later

steps.n: Changed root to make word meaning 'later/last' by adding ءَا at start and ِ (kasra) in middle

steps.m: the final life, what comes after
steps.n: Added feminine marker ة to create a noun meaning 'the final/later existence'
Added ٱلْ to make it definite (the specific Hereafter) and ِ because it's possessed by عَذَابُ (punishment) in عَذَابُ ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (punishment of the Hereafter)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word to show the quality itself rather than the action
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the end to show comparison. This word is comparing the punishment of the Hereafter to something else, showing it is stronger.
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form (meaning 'more' or 'most')
Added و at beginning to mean 'and', and added ٰ (alif khanjariyah) at end for proper writing of final alif
Added ـٓ (vertical small high mark) which is a pronunciation mark showing where to pause when reading
Question particle that turns a statement into a question

steps.m: And then is it? / And so did it?
steps.n: Adds فَ to show this question follows from previous statements
Adds لَمْ which negates the past tense. This combination of particles introduces the verb يَهْدِ (guided) that follows in the ayah.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: Added vowels to make the past tense form. The final ي changes to ى which is common for words ending in ي.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to kasra

steps.m: he guides (in a conditional or command context)
steps.n: The ي at the end is removed when giving a command or after certain words like 'if' or 'don't'
Added fatha to show the basic preposition meaning

steps.m: for them (referring to the previous generations mentioned in كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who the preposition is directed to. This connects to the previous generations being discussed in the ayah.

steps.m: how many (referring to أَهْلَكْنَا - we destroyed)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about quantity. In this ayah, it's asking about how many previous generations were destroyed, emphasizing the large number. It connects directly to أَهْلَكْنَا (we destroyed) which comes right after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from basic meaning 'to perish' to causative 'to cause to perish/destroy'
Adds subject pronoun 'we' to the verb
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable

steps.m: before them (referring to the previous nations mentioned in كَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا - how many We destroyed)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to. This connects to the previous part of the verse talking about destroyed nations.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from or originates from something else
The shadda (doubled م) adds emphasis to show importance
The fatha at the end connects this word to the next word الْقُرُونِ (the generations), showing it means 'from the generations'

steps.m: to join together in time or sequence

steps.n: Root meaning 'join together' becomes a noun meaning one group of people living in the same time period
Changed vowels and added و to make it plural
Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ِ because it comes after مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Added يَ prefix to show present tense, with sukun on first root letter and kasra on second root letter
Added ون suffix to show multiple people walking. This is happening in their dwellings (مَسٰكِنِهِمْ) according to the ayah.

steps.m: in (connects مَسٰكِنِهِمْ 'their dwellings' to يَمْشُونَ 'they walk')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects 'they walk' with 'their dwellings' to show where the walking happens.
Added مَ at start to show where something happens, like how 'write' becomes 'writing place'
Added ا after second letter and ِ before last letter to show many dwellings
Added ِ at end to show these dwellings belong to someone
Added هِمْ at end to show these are 'their' dwellings, and changed اَ to ٰ for easier pronunciation

Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes the statement stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English. Here it emphasizes that there are definitely signs/proofs (ءَايٰتٍ) in what was mentioned.
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something. In this ayah, it shows the people walking inside their dwellings.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby
The alif is replaced with a small alif above to extend the pointing distance

steps.m: that (pointing to something far) (refers to the previous statement about destroyed nations)
steps.n: Adding لِكَ changes it to point to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing back to the previous statement about how many generations were destroyed before them.
The root letters combine with the middle weak letter becoming a long ā sound
The ة shows this is one specific instance of a sign
Adding ات makes it plural, showing there are many signs
Adding لِ shows these signs belong to or are for something
Adding tanwin shows this is indefinite (not 'the' signs)
Starting with the basic combination of ل (for) and أولي (possessors/people of)

steps.m: for those who possess (connected to ءَايٰتٍ)
steps.n: Adding emphasis to the ل with a shadda. This word connects to ءَايٰتٍ (signs) in the ayah, showing these are signs for those who possess understanding.
Plural form of نُهْيَة meaning 'intelligence' - comes from root meaning 'to stop/prevent' since intelligence helps us know what to avoid. The ى shows it's plural like other words that end this way.
Added ال to point to specific intelligence, and the ن gets a shadda because it comes after ال
This is the basic conditional particle that expresses a hypothetical situation
Adding لا changes the meaning from a simple 'if' to 'if not for' which expresses that something would have happened if not for a preventing factor

steps.m: and if not for (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: The و connects this conditional statement to what came before it in the text
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from 'spoke' (verb) to 'word' (noun) by adding ة at the end and changing middle vowel to 'i'
The word gets a double-damma (ٌ) mark at the end to show it's doing the action of 'preceding' in this ayah

steps.m: to go ahead or come before something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word relates to كَلِمَةٌ (word) in this ayah.
The ت at the end shows that كَلِمَةٌ (which is feminine) is doing the action

steps.m: from (connects رَبِّكَ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something coming from a source or starting point. In this ayah, it connects 'your Lord' (رَبِّكَ) to what came before, showing where something originated.
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger and turns it into a noun
The kasra shows this word is owned by or connected to something that comes after it
Added كَ (meaning 'your') at the end to show the Lord belongs to you/the person being spoken to. Connected to مِن (from) earlier in the verse.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
The middle و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Adding لَ changes it from 'was' to 'would have been'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the pattern to create a noun meaning the result or effect of binding/making necessary

steps.m: as a binding necessity (predicate of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is the predicate (what something became) after كَانَ (was/would be)

steps.m: to set a time limit or deadline

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create a noun meaning the time limit itself. This word acts as a subject for كَانَ (would have been) in this verse.
Added و (and) to connect this with كَلِمَةٌ (word) from earlier in the verse, as they are both subjects of كَانَ
Added tanween damma (ٌ) to show this is indefinite, meaning 'a term' rather than 'the term'. This word pairs with مُّسَمًّى (appointed) in the next verse to mean 'an appointed term'.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make the root letters into 'he named' in past tense
The و at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end of the word
Added مُ at start and doubled the middle letter to show something received the action of naming
Added tanween (double fatha) to show it's indefinite - meaning 'a' named thing rather than 'the' named thing
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he was patient' to 'be patient!' by adding alif at start and changing vowels to make it a command

steps.m: so be patient! (in response to what they say)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a response or result. This connects to يَقُولُونَ (what they say) in the ayah.

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, about, or concerning something else. In this ayah, it connects 'be patient' (فَٱصْبِرْ) with 'what they say' (مَا يَقُولُونَ), meaning 'be patient concerning what they say'.
Added an alif symbol (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
This is a word used to refer to things or ask about them. In this ayah, it refers to the things that are being said.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels to ُ
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowels (fatha) to make past tense 'he glorified'
The doubled letter shows doing something a lot or with strength

steps.m: and glorify! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Adds 'wa' (and) at start + changes to a command form, connecting it with the previous command to be patient
Changed the action of praising into the thing itself - praise. Like how 'run' becomes 'running'.

steps.m: with praise (connected to رَبِّكَ - your Lord)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to mean 'with' and ِ at the end to show it connects to the next word (your Lord)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this an intensive form, emphasizing the complete nature of the nurturing
Adding kasra shows this word is connected to something else, like saying 'my Lord' or 'Lord of...'
Adding كَ shows the Lord belongs to the person being addressed. This word is possessed by حَمْدِ (praise) in the phrase بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ (with/by the praise of your Lord)

steps.m: to face or be in front of

steps.n: Changes verb 'to face' into noun meaning 'before'
Added fatha at the end to show this word owns what comes after it (طُلُوعِ meaning 'rising'). It's like saying 'before of rising' in Arabic.
Changed the word to show the action itself instead of doing the action. Like changing 'rise' to 'rising' in English.
Added ِ at the end to connect it to the next word (الشَّمْسِ - the sun), like saying 'rising of the sun'
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added ال to make it specific (like 'the' in English). The ش gets a shadda because it comes after ال.
Added kasra at the end because this word belongs to طُلُوعِ (rising), showing the relationship 'rising of the sun'
Forms a noun meaning 'before' from the root letters
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. The word relates to طُلُوعِ (rising) in this ayah, showing what comes before the rising.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added و to make it a noun meaning 'the act of setting'
Added ها meaning 'its' referring to الشَّمْسِ (the sun). The word comes after قَبْلَ (before) which requires the genitive case (shown by the kasra ِ)
This is a preposition meaning 'from' that connects to the word ءَانَآئِ (parts/hours) after it to show 'from the parts/hours'
Added و at beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. The full meaning is 'and from the parts/hours'

steps.m: to arrive or come at a specific time

steps.n: Changed from a verb about timing to a plural noun meaning 'periods/hours of time'
Added kasra to show this word connects to 'the night' (الَّيْلِ) that comes after it
Added ال at the beginning which causes the first letter to get a shadda (doubled)
The kasra shows this word is possessed by ءَانَآئِ (parts/hours) in the phrase 'parts of the night'
Adding vowels makes this a complete past tense verb meaning 'he glorified'
Changed to command form by adding shadda (double letter) and ending with sukoon
Adding a sukoon (stop mark) at the end of the command word
Adding فَ shows this action should happen after or because of what was mentioned before
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun
Changed to broken plural form meaning multiple edges
Added و meaning 'and' at beginning and fatha at end to show this word connects to النَّهَارِ (the day) coming after it
Added alif after second root letter to turn the concept of flowing light into the noun 'day'
Added ال at the beginning to make it definite, and doubled the ن with shadda because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the day (part of أَطْرَافَ ٱلنَّهَارِ - ends of the day)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word follows أَطْرَافَ (ends/parts) in a possessive structure meaning 'ends of the day'
This is a basic particle that expresses hope or expectation for something to happen

steps.m: perhaps you (will be satisfied with تَرْضَىٰ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this hope applies specifically to 'you'. This connects to the next word تَرْضَىٰ (will be satisfied) to complete the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Added ت at beginning to show 'you' and changed the end because it's a weak verb

steps.m: you will be pleased (referring to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added the long alif mark to show the extended sound at the end. This word is part of لَعَلَّكَ تَرْضَىٰ (so that you will be pleased)
Added fatha to show this is a complete word that negates the verb that follows it (تَمُدَّنَّ meaning 'extend')
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show doubled middle letter
Added ت prefix to show 'you' and changed vowel to show present tense. This forms a negative command because it comes after لا

steps.m: you must definitely not extend (negative command with لا)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to add strong emphasis to the negative command. The fatha before نَّ is needed for pronunciation.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes
steps.n: Root letters that give the basic meaning related to eyes and seeing
Added vowels (َ and ْ) to the root letters to make the word mean 'eye' instead of 'seeing'
Added the dual form ending to show exactly two eyes

steps.m: your two eyes (object of تَمُدَّنَّ - do not extend)
steps.n: Added the suffix that means 'your'. This word is being acted upon by تَمُدَّنَّ (do not extend) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It has no root since it's a primary particle.
Added an alif khanjariyah (small alif) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This connects to the word مَا which follows it in the ayah, showing what is being pointed towards.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects to the verb مَتَّعْنَا (we provided). It introduces what was provided.

steps.m: what/that which (connects to مَتَّعْنَا - we provided)
steps.n: Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation. The word introduces what Allah provided to others, connecting to the verb مَتَّعْنَا (we provided).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger, showing he gave a lot of enjoyment
Adding نا at the end shows 'we' did the action instead of 'he'
Added kasra to the letter ب to show it's a preposition meaning 'with'

steps.m: with it/him (referring to what was given to others)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه with kasra to show what/who is being referred to

steps.m: with it/him (referring to what was given to others)
steps.n: Added small alif (ۦ) and elongation mark (ٓ) as pronunciation helpers

steps.m: to pair or join things together

steps.n: Root letters combined to make the noun meaning one spouse/partner
Changes from single to plural by adding 'a' at start and 'aa' in middle - like changing 'spouse' to 'spouses'

steps.m: pairs/spouses (as object of مَتَّعْنَا - we provided)
steps.n: Added tanween fatḥa to show this is the object of the verb مَتَّعْنَا (we provided) in this ayah
Basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added emphasis to the م with a shadda because it follows a word ending in a vowel

steps.m: from them (referring to the worldly pleasures mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُمْ (them) to show who the preposition refers to
Changed the root letters into a noun by adding vowel marks to mean 'flower' or 'beauty'
Added ة to show one specific instance of splendor
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb متَّعْنَا (we gave them to enjoy)
Changed from root meaning 'to live' into a noun meaning 'living' by adding و in the middle
Added ة to make the word feminine, changing it from 'living' to 'life'
Added ٱلْ (the) at the start to make it 'the life' instead of just 'life'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to زَهْرَةَ (splendor) in the phrase 'splendor of the worldly life'
Root د-ن-و means 'near' - added vowels and ي to make the word for 'worldly life' or 'this world'
Added ال to make it specific. The د gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل. This word describes 'life' (الْحَيَوٰةِ) in the phrase 'worldly life'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adds first person plural subject pronoun
Added لِ to show purpose - this explains why or for what purpose we're doing the testing

steps.m: so that we may test them (referring to those who are attracted to worldly pleasures mentioned in the ayah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being tested - refers back to those mentioned in the previous part of the verse who are attracted to worldly decorations
Added kasra (ِ) to show it's a preposition

steps.m: in it (referring to what We have provided as enjoyment)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه with kasra (هِ) at the end to show what/who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to what Allah has provided as temporary enjoyment.
Changes from action to the thing itself (like 'provide' to 'provision')
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this word is the subject (the doer) in the sentence
Adds 'and' to connect this word with the previous phrase about worldly splendor (زَهْرَةَ)

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who completely and perfectly does the caring and developing. Added fatha to show it's a noun.
Added kasra to show this Lord belongs to something
Added كَ (ka) at the end to mean 'your Lord' - showing it belongs to you (if speaking to one male). The word رِزْقُ (provision) owns/controls this word in the sentence.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to create the basic adjective form. This word describes رِزْقُ (provision) in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is describing something. Here it's telling us that your Lord's provision is better than worldly pleasures.
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make comparative form (meaning 'more' or 'most')
Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. This word is being compared to رِزْقُ (provision) in this ayah.
Makes it into a command form telling one person to do something
Adds و with fatha to connect this command with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to make it a noun meaning 'family'

steps.m: your family (object of وَأْمُرْ - and command)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show it belongs to 'you'. This word is what's being acted on by the command وَأْمُرْ (and command) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from 'to pray' into 'one prayer' by adding ة at the end
Added ال to make it specific

steps.m: with/by the prayer (object of وَأْمُرْ - and command)
steps.n: Added بِ (meaning 'with/by') at start and kasra ending because it's being acted upon by the command word وَأْمُرْ (meaning 'command')
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif and changing vowels to make it a command form
Added ط (ta) to make the word stronger - means to force yourself to be extra patient

steps.m: and endure! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous phrase about ordering family to pray
Starting with the basic preposition meaning 'upon'
The ى changes to يْ when adding a suffix

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to الصَّلَوٰةِ - the prayer)
steps.n: Added ها which means 'it/her' to show what is being acted upon. Here it refers back to 'the prayer' mentioned earlier in the verse.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb نَسْـَٔلُكَ (we ask you) negative, changing the meaning to 'we do not ask you'
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Adding ن at the start shows that 'we' are doing the action
Adding كَ shows you are the one being asked. The hamza (ـَٔ) is written differently here because it comes after a sukun.

steps.m: to provide or sustain with resources

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter

steps.m: provision (object of نَسْـَٔلُكَ - we ask you for)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being asked for in the phrase 'we do not ask you for provision'
This is a standalone personal pronoun meaning 'we', not derived from any root. In this ayah, it emphasizes that it is specifically 'we' who provide, connecting to the next word نَرْزُقُ (we provide).
Added shadda (doubled ن) to emphasize 'we', making it stronger like saying 'indeed we' or 'truly we'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from past tense (razaqa) to present tense (yarzuqu) by changing vowel pattern
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added كَ at the end to show you are receiving the action

steps.m: to come after, to result from something

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it a noun meaning 'result'
Added the definite article 'the'
Added conjunction 'and' to connect with previous phrase
Added final damma to show this is the subject of the sentence, and changed alif to alif madd for proper writing
Changes from verb 'to protect' into the noun 'piety'
Added ال (the) at beginning which causes the ت to get a shadda
Added لِ (meaning 'for' or 'to') at the beginning to show purpose or direction
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add the past tense pattern. This is a common change with middle-weak verbs.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (for a group of three or more)
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
This is the first part of the compound particle expressing a conditional meaning
Adding لا changes the meaning from a simple 'if' to express wondering or wishing why something hasn't happened. This is connected to قَالُوا (they said) showing what the people were asking about.
Changed root أتي into present tense verb by adding يَ at start and vowels to make يَأْتِي (he comes)
Added نَا at the end to show who receives the action (us)

steps.m: to show, to be clear, to be apparent

steps.n: The root letters combine with a long alif (آ) and the ة at the end turns the general meaning 'to show' into a specific 'sign' or 'miracle'
The preposition بِ shows this sign is being used as a tool or means

steps.m: with a sign (object of يَأْتِينَا - he comes to us)
steps.n: The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (any sign). This sign is what's being brought in the phrase 'he comes to us with a sign'
This is a preposition that shows origin or source

steps.m: from (connects رَّبِّهِ to what came before)
steps.n: Added shadda (doubled م) because it comes after a pause. In this ayah, it connects 'their Lord' (رَّبِّهِ) to what came before, showing where the sign/miracle would come from.
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action completely
Adding shadda at start for emphasis because it follows a word ending in a vowel
Adding kasra shows this word is possessed by or connected to something after it
Adding هِ shows possession (his), with ۦٓ as a pronunciation helper
Adding fatha to the interrogative hamza shows this is a question
Adding و with fatha connects this question to what came before it
Adding لَمْ makes the question about something that hasn't happened in the past
Connected the root letters together
Added ت at beginning to make it present tense, and ي at end to show it's for she/it
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added هِم at the end to show the action affects 'them' (masculine plural)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Adding ة makes it into a specific instance of clear evidence
The ُ shows this is the subject doing the action in تَأْتِهِم (come to them)

steps.m: that which (is written) (connects to فِى ٱلصُّحُفِ - in the scriptures)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun meaning 'that which' in this context. It connects what was mentioned before to what comes after in the scriptures.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or time. It doesn't come from a root.
Added the kasra vowel mark under the ف to show how it's pronounced.
Changed the ي to ى at the end. This is just a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it shows location within 'the scriptures' (الصُّحُفِ).
Added damma vowels to create the plural form of the word
Added the definite article ٱل and shadda on ص because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after فِى (in) in the phrase مَا فِى ٱلصُّحُفِ (what is in the scrolls)
Added damma to create the basic adjective form
Added ى and fatha to make it feminine and emphasize it's the foremost or primary one
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular previous scriptures mentioned in the ayah
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation. This particle introduces hypothetical situations that didn't happen.

steps.m: and even if/although (connects this hypothetical situation to what came before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a particle that introduces and emphasizes what comes after it, like saying 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English
Added 'we' by attaching نا (meaning 'we') to أنّ, but written as آ to make it flow better. Like adding 'we' to 'that' to make 'that we'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to change from 'perishing' to 'causing to perish' (destroying)
Added نا to show 'we' did the action
Added هُم to show who was destroyed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Long ا after second root letter makes this a noun meaning the result of punishment. The word is in genitive case because it comes after the preposition ب (with/by).
Adding بِ at the beginning shows this punishment is being used as a tool or means
The tanween (double kasra) shows this is any punishment, not a specific one
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن, causing the م to be doubled in pronunciation. This is a pronunciation rule and doesn't change the meaning.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by عَذَابٍ (punishment) and comes after the word مِن (from/of)
Added the pronoun هِ (with optional ۦ) at the end to show 'it' referring back to عَذَابٍ (punishment)
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has و in middle). This is a common pattern in Arabic.
Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural)

steps.m: they would surely say (in response to أَهْلَكْنٰهُم - if We had destroyed them)
steps.n: Adding لَ shows this is the result of a condition (if something had happened). In this ayah, it's saying what they would have said if Allah had destroyed them.
Added fatha to first letter and doubled the middle letter to show this is someone who does this action completely

steps.m: our Lord (being addressed in قَالُوا they said)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this belongs to us. This word is what is being said after قَالُوا (they said).
This is a conditional particle that shows something might have happened but didn't
Adding لا changes the meaning to express a wish or desire about something that didn't happen

steps.m: if not, why not (expressing wish) (connected to أَرْسَلْتَ - you sent)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to show connection with the next word. In this ayah, it expresses a wish about why a messenger wasn't sent.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the start and sukun on س makes the verb causative - showing someone made the action happen
Adding تَ suffix changes the verb from third person to second person masculine singular
This is a preposition showing direction
Added sukun before attaching pronoun
Added نا which means 'us'. In this ayah, it shows who the messenger should be sent to in the phrase 'why did you not send to us a messenger'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و

steps.m: a messenger (object of أَرْسَلْتَ - you sent)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent in the phrase 'why did you not send to us a messenger'

steps.m: to follow closely or go after

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing vowels
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from previous statement
Added a shadda (doubled letter) to make the meaning stronger - like 'really follow' instead of just 'follow'
Changed the ending to show this is connected to something before it
Changed root letters to form singular noun meaning 'sign'. The hamza changed to ء with fatha, and the first ي changed to ا.
Added feminine plural ending ات with alif changed to alif maksura (ٰ)
Added second person singular possessive suffix كَ with kasra connector. This word is the object of نَتَّبِعَ (we follow) in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with قَبْلِ (before) to mean 'from before'.
Added the kasra (ِ) mark under the م to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'min'
Changes from root to noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the preposition مِن which requires the genitive case
This is a basic particle that connects parts of sentences, similar to 'that' in English. Here it introduces what the people would say.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the أ to show the correct pronunciation 'an'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'was humiliated' to a command meaning 'become humiliated' by changing first vowel and adding emphasis mark (double letter)

steps.m: we become humiliated (connected to أَن before it)
steps.n: Added ن at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. This word comes after أَن (that) in the ayah, showing it's part of what might happen.
The ن gets a shadda because it comes after أَن in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' and changed vowels for present tense. The ى at the end is because this is a weak verb (ends in ي)
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
Added alif maqsurah (ى) at the end to complete the verb form
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed from 'he said' to 'say!' by dropping first vowel and changing middle vowel to 'oo' sound
The middle weak letter و is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Adding damma and doubling the ل creates a word that means 'everything without exception'
The double damma (tanween) shows this word is the subject of the sentence. It connects to the next word فَتَرَبَّصُوا (so wait) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels

steps.m: he waited and watched intensively (by himself)
steps.n: Adding ت at start and doubling middle letter shows doing action to oneself intensively

steps.m: one who waits and watches intensively
steps.n: Changed to active participle form to show who does the action

steps.m: one who waits and watches intensively (subject of قُلْ)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and tanween to show this is the subject of the verb قُلْ (say)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he waited watchfully (for something to happen)
steps.n: Adding تَ at the beginning and doubling the middle letter shows doing the action more intensely and deliberately
The فَ shows this is a response or result of what came before. Here it responds to the command 'say' (قُلْ)

steps.m: so all of you wait watchfully (command to multiple people)
steps.n: Adding وا makes this a command to a group
Changed vowels to prepare for present tense form
Adding ت changes it to 'you' form in present tense
Adding ونَ makes it refer to a group of people instead of just one person

steps.m: who (asking about the people who are أَصْحٰبُ ٱلصِّرٰطِ ٱلسَّوِىِّ)
steps.n: This is a question word that asks about people. In this ayah, it's asking about who are the companions of the straight path.
Added vowels to make basic past tense verb
Changed to active participle pattern فَاعِل to show who does the action
Changed to broken plural pattern أَفْعَال to show multiple companions
Added a damma (u-sound) at the end to show these companions are doing the action, and changed the middle alif to a longer alif
The pattern فِعَال is used for nouns meaning paths or ways
Adding definite article makes it refer to a specific path

steps.m: of the path (possessed by أَصْحٰبُ - people/companions)
steps.n: Adds kasra at end to show genitive case. This word is in genitive case because it is possessed by the word أَصْحٰبُ (companions/people), forming the phrase 'people of the path'.

steps.m: to be straight, level, or equal

steps.n: Added vowels to make a basic adjective showing a quality
Adding shadda makes the quality stronger
Adding ال makes it specific

steps.m: the very straight/proper one (referring to the path mentioned before)
steps.n: Changed the starting 'a' to a different form, and adjusted the ending spelling marks - but meaning stays the same
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to what comes after it (اهْتَدَىٰ - guided)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added اِتْ to change from 'guiding others' to 'finding guidance for oneself'
The ى at the end shows this is a past tense verb
